data.title: A Look At Region Group’s Valuation As It Completes And Extends Its Share Buyback Program
data.description:
Region Group (ASX:RGN) has completed its previously announced on market share buyback, repurchasing 14,100,000 shares for A$33.1 million, and extended the program’s duration through April 2, 2027, underscoring ongoing capital management efforts.

See our latest analysis for Region Group.

At a latest share price of A$2.23, Region Group’s recent 1-day and 7-day share price returns in positive territory contrast with weaker 90 day and year to date share price returns. The 1 year total shareholder return remains positive, suggesting near term momentum has eased after a stronger period for patient holders.

If this kind of capital management story has you looking wider, it could be a good moment to scan other ideas through the 4 top founder-led companies

With Region Group trading at A$2.23 alongside an estimated intrinsic value gap and a discount to analyst price targets, the question is whether this REIT is quietly undervalued or if the market is already pricing in future growth.

Advertisement

Price to Earnings of 8.2x: Is it justified?

Region Group is trading on a P/E of 8.2x, which screens as good value against both its estimated fair P/E of 13.5x and the global retail REITs average of 13x, even though it sits above the closer peer group average of 7.1x.

The P/E ratio compares the share price to earnings per share, so it reflects what investors are willing to pay today for each dollar of current earnings. For a REIT like Region Group, where earnings are tied to rental income from long leases with grocery anchored centres, this metric is often a quick way to gauge how the market is pricing its cash generating power.

In this context, the current 8.2x multiple is lower than the SWS fair P/E estimate of 13.5x. This is a level the market could move towards if sentiment or cash flow expectations were to converge with that fair ratio. Against the wider global retail REITs group at 13x, Region Group screens as cheaper. However, compared with its more direct peer average of 7.1x it looks a little richer. This suggests investors may already be paying a premium versus local peers while still sitting at a discount to broader REIT valuations and the modelled fair P/E.

data.title: How Investors May Respond To Ascendis Pharma (ASND) Growth‑Disorder Data And New Board Appointment
data.description:
• In March 2026, Ascendis Pharma reported positive new data for its TransCon portfolio, including Week 52 Phase 2 results for TransCon hGH in Turner syndrome and two-year ApproaCH Trial outcomes for TransCon CNP in achondroplasia, while also adding former BioMarin CEO Jean‑Jacques Bienaimé to its board as a non‑executive director.
  • The combination of once‑weekly therapies showing efficacy and safety comparable to existing daily standards, plus experienced rare‑disease leadership, highlights Ascendis’s push to strengthen its position in growth‑disorder treatments.
  • We’ll now explore how these TransCon hGH and YUVIWEL data updates may influence Ascendis Pharma’s investment narrative and long‑term outlook.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 33 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10b in market cap - there's still time to get in early.

Advertisement

Ascendis Pharma Investment Narrative Recap

To own Ascendis Pharma, you need to believe its TransCon platform can turn a small set of rare endocrine drugs into a durable, multi-indication franchise while controlling cash burn.
